prs.title: feat(helm): add extraCommandArgs support to remaining components

labels.name: kind/feature
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a new feature.

labels.name: size/L
labels.description: Denotes a PR that changes 100-499 lines, ignoring generated files.

prs.body:
## What type of PR is this?

/kind feature

## What this PR does / why we need it

This PR adds `extraCommandArgs` support to all remaining Helm chart components:

- `karmada-scheduler`
- `karmada-descheduler`
- `karmada-scheduler-estimator`
- `karmada-webhook`
- `karmada-agent`
- `karmada-aggregated-apiserver`
- `karmada-apiserver`
- `karmada-metrics-adapter`
- `karmada-search`

Each component now exposes:

```yaml
extraCommandArgs: {}
```

Arguments are rendered as:

```yaml
- --<key>=<value>
```

and are appended after built-in and dedicated flags. This matches the existing `controllerManager` behavior and intentionally allows `extraCommandArgs` to act as the final override layer for single-value flags.

Documentation has also been added to `values.yaml` describing the precedence behavior and noting that some multi-value flags may not behave as simple overrides.

## Which issue(s) this PR fixes

Fixes #7626

## Special notes for your reviewer

Validation performed:

```bash
helm lint charts/karmada
helm template charts/karmada >/dev/null
```

Additionally, rendering was verified with sample `extraCommandArgs` values (including `schedulerEstimator`) to ensure `--key=value` arguments are correctly appended to manifests.

prs.title: fix: close pie menus on first canvas click

labels.name: bug fix
labels.description: Fixes a bug or incorrect behavior

prs.body:
fixes #7545

- [x] BUG FIX

## DESCRIPTION
Pie menus like the pitch selector and numeric selector do not close when the user clicks the nearby canvas area or the grey background circle. The user has to click twice, or click far away from the menu, to dismiss it.

## Root cause

The #wheelDiv DOM overlay is an absolutely positioned container (400px square, z-index 20000) sitting on top of the CreateJS canvas. It displays a grey SVG background circle that extends around the pie menu slices. Any click inside this square is captured by the DOM layer and never reaches the canvas beneath, so the CreateJS stagemousedown handler that normally closes menus is never called.

## Changes

`activeExitWheel` module variable
Tracks the currently open menu exit wheel instance so the outside click handler knows which exit routine to invoke. Set inside configureExitWheel which runs for every pie menu type, so no per menu wiring is needed.

`isInteractive(target)` function
Determines whether a click target belongs to an interactive element that should keep the menu open. First checks the #labelDiv container that houses the text and numeric input field so typing into the input does not dismiss the menu. Then inspects #wheelDiv SVG slices and titles by checking cursor pointer style, both inline and computed, with a try/catch guard for environments where getComputedStyle is unavailable.

`handleOutsideClick(event)` function
The document mousedown listener. Early returns if #wheelDiv is hidden. If the click target is not interactive, calls the active exit wheel navigateFunction to run the proper exit routine which may include updating block text, cleaning up Raphael wheels, and hiding the div. Falls back to a direct hideWheelDiv call when no exit wheel reference is available.

`showWheelDiv()` changes
Wraps the document mousedown listener registration in a 50ms setTimeout. This prevents the mousedown that opened the menu from being captured by the handler and immediately closing it again. Guards against adding the listener if the div was already hidden by the time the timeout fires.

`hideWheelDiv()` changes
Removes the mousedown listener and nulls out activeExitWheel so the handler will not fire after the menu is gone. Fixes the duplicate docById call by using the local wheelDiv reference.

`configureExitWheel()` change
Sets activeExitWheel to the newly configured exit wheel. Since every pie menu calls configureExitWheel during setup, every menu type gets tracked without per menu changes.

prs.title: fix: set XIsResourceName for immutable rule API calls during force project delete
prs.body:
Fixes #981
When deleting a project with a numeric name using force, the immutable rule APIs were not telling Harbor to treat the value as a project name. Harbor interpreted it as an ID instead, so the CLI failed to find and remove the immutable rules. This caused repository deletion to fail with an HTTP 412 error and blocked the entire project deletion.

The fix sets XIsResourceName true on ListImmutable and DeleteImmutable API calls so Harbor correctly resolves the value as a project name, allowing projects with numeric names to be force deleted successfully.

prs.title: fix(sdk): resolve git context from CI/platform environment variables
prs.body: fixes # https://github.com/traceroot-ai/traceroot/issues/1024 Summary This PR fixes an issue where the Python SDK could not resolve git_repo and git_ref in production environments that do not include a .git directory or git binary. A new CI/CD environment variable resolution layer has been added before local git auto-detection. Changes * Added harvest_ci_git_context(env=None) for CI/CD platform metadata. * Wired resolution as: explicit args -> TRACEROOT_GIT_* -> CI/platform env -> local git -> warn once * Added warn-once behavior when git context remains unresolved. * Kept unresolved fields omitted instead of fabricating values. * Added dedicated tests covering CI platform resolution and warning behavior. ## Checklist - [x] I have added/updated tests where applicable - [x] I have updated documentation where necessary --- ## Summary by cubic Resolve `git_repo` and `git_ref` in CI/CD by harvesting platform environment variables, so the Python SDK works in production without a `.git` directory or `git` binary. Adds a one-time, thread-safe warning if both values remain unresolved, and avoids fabricating defaults. - **Bug Fixes** - Added CI/platform env harvesting (`harvest_ci_git_context`) for GitHub Actions, Vercel, GitLab CI, CircleCI, Bitbucket, and Render. - Per-field resolution order: explicit args → `TRACEROOT_GIT_*` → CI/platform env → local git; unresolved fields remain None. - Emits a warning with a docs link only when both values are unresolved, guarded to fire once per process. - Made the warning guard thread-safe to prevent duplicate logs in multi-threaded apps. Written for commit 9fd655c282236b9bcf931cb6e564fab7b03f613c. Summary will update on new commits.

prs.title: fix(detectors): support BYOK models for automatic RCA
prs.body: Fixes #991 ## Summary ## Type of Change - [x] fix - A bug fix ## Details Allowing automatic detector RCA sessions to resolve and use workspace BYOK/custom models instead of only models present in SYSTEM_MODELS. Previously, when a project was configured with a BYOK model such as: * deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3 * gemini-2.5-flash the detector RCA worker failed to resolve the model, omitted model, providerName, and source from the agent request, and the agent service fell back to the default Claude model. ## Root Cause resolveSystemModel() only checked the static SYSTEM_MODELS registry. ## Changes * Replaced resolveSystemModel() with resolveProjectModel() * Added support for resolving workspace BYOK models through modelProvider * Preserved existing behavior for system models * Added tests covering: * system model resolution * BYOK model resolution * unknown model fallback * null/undefined model handling ## Screenshots / Recordings (if applicable) https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0a54fbf3-6394-4c3c-a36a-f0eee960c5d2 ## Checklist - [x] I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md) - [x] I have added/updated tests where applicable - [x] I have added screenshots or recordings for UI changes (if applicable) - [x] I have updated documentation where necessary --- ## Summary by cubic Fixes automatic RCA not using workspace BYOK models by resolving and sending the chosen model, provider, and source end-to-end. Also enforces free-plan RCA caps so blocked workspaces don’t run RCA. - **Bug Fixes** - Replaced resolver with async resolveProjectModel(rcaModel, rcaProvider, rcaSource, workspaceId) using `@traceroot/core/model-resolver` (fetchProviderConfig + resolvePiModel); supports system/BYOK, returns ModelSource.SYSTEM or ModelSource.BYOK, and forwards the BYOK provider label to the agent. - Made the legacy BYOK fallback deterministic (provider lookup ordered by id) and added error handling; tests cover duplicate provider resolution and DB error path. - Removed prefix-based provider guessing and catalog inference; BYOK now requires a saved provider in the workspace or resolves via legacy fallback, otherwise returns null. - Persisted rcaProvider and rcaSource on Project; exposed via GET/PATCH APIs (including `/api/projects/[projectId]`) and wired into Detectors settings; worker reads these in processRcaJob and includes them in runRcaSession. Added tests for routes, runRcaSession, and processRcaJob covering system/BYOK resolution, missing provider, unknown/empty models, free-plan skip, and failure path. - **Migration** - Adds rca_provider and rca_source columns; run DB migrations. No manual backfill needed due to the legacy BYOK fallback. Written for commit 1b20e485e592fe6a6d2c9b8155c7250f097080d9. Summary will update on new commits.

prs.title: fix(registryctl): avoid fatal log on graceful shutdown
prs.body:
## Comprehensive Summary of your change

This PR fixes a misleading fatal log emitted by registryctl during graceful shutdown.

Previously, http.ErrServerClosed returned from ListenAndServe() was treated as a fatal error and logged as:

 [FATAL] http: Server closed 

This behavior occurs during normal graceful shutdown and should not be considered a fatal error.

The fix explicitly ignores http.ErrServerClosed before calling log.Fatal.

# Issue being fixed

Fixes #23294

This PR fixes an issue where the calendar automatically jumps to the next month when there are no available slots in the current month, preventing users from navigating back.

prs.title: Add comprehensive test suite for weight_function module
prs.body: ## Overview Added a comprehensive test suite for the `weight_function` module with **25 test cases** covering all functions. Python tests are now integrated into `npm run test` so they run alongside existing JavaScript unit tests. --- ## Changes Made ### `package.json` Modified npm test scripts to include Python `weight_function` tests. #### Updates - The `test` script now runs **Vue.js unit tests** and **Python tests** sequentially - Added a new `test:weights` script with Python environment optimizations ```json { "test": "vue-cli-service test:unit && npm run test:weights", "test:weights": "PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m pytest weight_function/tests/ -v -p no:cacheprovider" } ``` ### Explanation - **`PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1`** Prevents creation of the `__pycache__` directory and `.pyc` bytecode files. - **`python3`** Explicitly uses Python 3 (commonly required for compatibility on macOS and modern environments). - **`-v`** Enables verbose output, showing detailed test results. - **`-p no:cacheprovider`** Disables pytest’s cache provider, preventing generation of `.pytest_cache`. ### weight_function/main.py Fixed division by zero warning in calculate_eigen() function: ``` # Before CR = CI / RI # After if RI == 0: CR = 0.0 else: CR = CI / RI ``` ## New Files ### `weight_function/tests/test_main.py` (7 tests) Core unit tests covering basic functions: - **`TestCalculateEigen`** – 4 tests for eigenvalue calculations - **`TestInitializeAhpMatrix`** – 3 tests for AHP matrix initialization --- ### `weight_function/tests/test_main_extended.py` (18 tests) Extended test coverage for remaining functions: - **`TestGenerateSaatyScale`** – 5 tests for Saaty scale generation - **`TestFillAhpMatrix`** – 6 tests for matrix filling operations - **`TestPopulateAhpMatrix`** – 7 tests for AHP matrix population --- ### npm run test ### npm run test:weights

prs.title: Fix: handle missing pre-test and post-test sections in task completion logic
prs.body:
## Overview
Fix a critical bug in the moderated test where completing tasks fails with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'value')` when the test structure doesn't include pre-test or post-test sections.

## Problem Statement
When a test is created without pre-test or post-test sections, the `items.value` array structure changes:
- **With pre-test**: `items.value[0]` = Pre-test, `items.value[1]` = Tasks
- **Without pre-test**: `items.value[0]` = Tasks (index mismatch!)

The `completeStep()` function was hardcoded to access `items.value[1]` for tasks, causing undefined reference errors when pre-test or post-test are missing.

## Solution
Added safe optional chaining (`?.`) checks before accessing nested `items.value` properties:

prs.title: Fix: serialise proxy objects when saving pre-post test variables
prs.body:
## Description
Fixes issue #1485 where pre-test and post-test variables are not initialized after pressing the calibration config confirmation button.

## Root Cause
1. Vue 3's reactivity system wraps reactive arrays/objects in Proxy objects. When these Proxy objects were directly dispatched to the Vuex store and saved to Firebase, they couldn't be properly serialized, causing data loss.

2. Missing Vuex namespace prefix in store dispatch/getter calls - actions were not being found in the UserStudy module.

3. Vuex store was not being cleared when switching between different tests, causing data from old tests to appear in new tests.

## Solution
1. Convert Vue Proxy objects to plain JavaScript objects using structuredClone() at two critical points:
   - When loading from database (deserialization)
   - When saving to database (serialization)

2. Add proper Vuex namespace prefix to all store dispatch/getter calls:
   - Use `store.dispatch('UserStudy/setPreTest', data)` instead of `store.dispatch('setPreTest', data)`
   - Use `store.getters['UserStudy/preTest']` instead of `store.getters.preTest`

## Changes Made
- Updated getTasks() to use structuredClone(tasksData)
- Updated getPreTest() to use structuredClone(preTestData)
- Updated getPostTest() to use structuredClone(postTestData)
- Updated save() function to use structuredClone() for testStructure serialization
- Fixed UserVariables emit dispatch to use 'UserStudy/setPreTest' and 'UserStudy/setPostTest' namespace

prs.title: Fixed and move account deletion logic from view to controller
prs.body:
fixes #1050 
resubmission #1301 
## Summary
 Logic is moved from ProfileView.vue to AuthController for better maintainability, testability, and reusability. Users can delete their accounts safely by verifying their identity through either email/password or Google authentication.
## Changes
### AuthController.js
**New Methods Added:**
- **`deleteUserAccount(user, password)`**: Core deletion logic
  - Detects provider type (Google or email/password)
  - Reauthenticates user based on their sign-in method
    - Email/password users: Uses password credential
    - Google users: Shows Google OAuth popup
  - Deletes Firebase Auth user account
  - Calls backend cleanup endpoint
  - Automatically signs out user after deletion
  
- **`deleteAuth(userId)`**: Backend cleanup endpoint call
  - Posts to Firebase Cloud Function to clean up Firestore and storage
  - Logs errors without throwing (non-critical step)
### ProfileView.vue
- **Step 1 - Confirmation**:
  - `handlerDeleteConfirmText()`: 
    - Validates user is still logged in
    - Detects if user has email/password provider (needed for password field)
    - Moves to Step 2 of deletion flow
- **Step 2 - Authentication**:
  - Shows password field only for email/password users
  - Shows Google auth message for Google-only users
  - `handlerDeleteAccount()`:
    - Gets password (if email user) or null (if Google user)
    - Calls `authController.deleteUserAccount()` with appropriate credentials
    - Redirects to home page on success

prs.title: Fixes: Prevent admins to overwrite changes in study settings
prs.body:
How It Works (Flow)

When multiple admins are editing the Study Settings at the same time:
1. Admin A updates the Title and saves.
2. Admin B already has the settings open and updates the Description.
3. Since Admin A’s changes were saved earlier, the system detects that Admin B is working with an outdated version.
4. On saving, the web app shows a “Conflicts detected” warning.
5. The system automatically:
      Retains Admin A’s updated Title
      Saves Admin B’s updated Description
      Ensures that no previously saved changes are lost

issues.title: [BUG] Pie menu does not close on first canvas click
issues.body:
## Describe the bug

When a pie menu (such as piemenuPitches or piemenuNumber) is open, clicking on the non-interactive area around the menu (for example, the grey circular background surrounding the wheel) does not close the menu on the first click.

The menu remains visible even though the user clicked outside the interactive wheel items. Users often need to click a second time or click farther away before the menu is dismissed.

## To Reproduce

1. Open any pie menu (pitch selector, number selector, etc.).
2. Click near the wheel are.
3. Observe that the menu remains open.
4. Click little bit away from wheel it closes.
5. The menu is finally dismissed.

## Expected behavior

Clicking anywhere outside the interactive wheel elements should immediately dismiss the currently open pie menu.

## Root cause

Pie menus are rendered inside a high z-index DOM overlay (#wheelDiv) positioned above the CreateJS canvas.

While interactive wheel elements (slices, labels, and controls) handle their own actions correctly, clicks on non-interactive regions of the wheel container are not treated as dismissal events.

Because these clicks are captured by the DOM overlay instead of reaching the canvas-based dismissal logic, the active wheel remains open even though the user has effectively clicked outside the menu content.

## Environment

* Browser: Chrome, Firefox
* OS: All
* Version: master (latest)

issues.title: ResourceDetector does not retry policy claim updates on conflicts

labels.name: kind/feature
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a new feature.

issues.body:
## Description

### What happened?

`ClaimPolicyForObject()` and `ClaimClusterPolicyForObject()` update resource templates using a direct `Client.Update()` call without conflict retries.

```go

AddPPClaimMetadata(object, policyID, policy.ObjectMeta)

return policyID, d.Client.Update(context.TODO(), object)

```

```go

AddCPPClaimMetadata(object, policyID, policy.ObjectMeta)

return policyID, d.Client.Update(context.TODO(), object)

```

When a resource template is modified concurrently by another controller or user, these updates may fail with a conflict error and abort the reconciliation.

### What did you expect to happen?

Policy claim updates should retry on optimistic concurrency conflicts and refresh the latest object state before retrying, similar to other metadata update paths in the ResourceDetector.

issues.title: bug: CronFHPAScaleTargetRefUpdates does not update cached scale target after change detection

labels.name: kind/bug
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a bug.

issues.body:
### Problem

`CronFHPAScaleTargetRefUpdates()` detects when a `CronFederatedHPA` scale target changes, but it does not update the cached target reference stored in `cronFHPAScaleTargetMap`.

### Reproduction

Example sequence:

1. Register target A.
2. Update to target B.
3. Call the function again with target B.

Expected:

target A -> false
target B -> true
target B -> false

Actual:

target A -> false
target B -> true
target B -> true

### Root Cause

The cached target stored in `cronFHPAScaleTargetMap` is only initialized on the first call and is never updated when a new target is detected.

Because of this, the old target remains in the cache and every subsequent comparison continues to detect a difference.

### Expected Behavior

After detecting a target change, the cache should be updated to store the new target reference so future calls compare against the latest state.


### Verification

A unit test reproduces the issue:

1. Initialize the cache with target A.
2. Update to target B.
3. Call the function again with target B.

The test fails on the current implementation because the third call returns `true` instead of `false`.

After updating the cached target reference, the test passes successfully.


- Component: pkg/controllers/cronfederatedhpa
- Function: CronFHPAScaleTargetRefUpdates

issues.title: registryctl logs http: Server closed as fatal during graceful shutdown
issues.body:
# Issue Title

registryctl logs http: Server closed as fatal during graceful shutdown

# Description

## Summary

registryctl logs a fatal error during normal graceful shutdown:


However, this is caused by http.ErrServerClosed, which is expected behavior when the HTTP server shuts down gracefully.

## Current Behavior

In RegistryCtl.Start():

err = regCtl.ListenAndServe() 
... 
if err != nil {     log.Fatal(err) } 

ListenAndServe() returns http.ErrServerClosed after a successful shutdown, causing Harbor to incorrectly emit a fatal log.

## Expected Behavior

Graceful shutdown should not produce fatal logs.

http.ErrServerClosed should be ignored explicitly.

## Result

After applying the fix:

- graceful shutdown logs are clean
- no misleading fatal message appears
- shutdown behavior remains unchanged

issues.title: Feature Request: Add Google Sheets Adapter Support
issues.body:
## Summary

Add support for Google Sheets integration in Clawvisor via a new adapter:

```txt

google.sheets

```

This would extend the existing Google ecosystem integrations (`gmail`, `calendar`, `drive`, `contacts`) and unlock many useful agent workflows involving structured/tabular data.

---

## Why This Would Be Valuable

Google Sheets is one of the most commonly used productivity tools for:

- analytics tracking
- logging agent outputs
- CRM-style workflows
- financial tracking
- lightweight databases
- reporting pipelines
- collaborative data management

AI agents frequently need to:

- append rows
- read structured ranges
- update cells
- generate reports
- maintain state across workflows

Since Clawvisor already supports:

- Google OAuth
- Google credential handling
- Google Drive integration

adding Sheets support should integrate naturally into the existing architecture.

---

## Proposed Service

```txt
google.sheets
```
---
## Suggested Initial Actions (MVP)

```txt
list_spreadsheets
get_spreadsheet
read_range
append_rows
update_cells
create_spreadsheet
```
---

## Possible Future Actions

```txt

batch_update
create_sheet_tab
delete_sheet_tab
format_cells
formula_support
share_spreadsheet

```

---

---

## Additional Notes

This appears to be one of the largest missing integrations in the current adapter ecosystem, especially given existing support for Google Drive and other productivity platforms.

Would be happy to contribute or help test if interested.

issues.title: Remove temporary SBOM permission override in ArtifactListPageService
issues.body:
## Description

In `artifact-list-page.service.ts`, _hasSbomPermission is already populated using the result returned from:

```ts

hasProjectPermissions(projectId, permissions)

```

However, the value is immediately overridden by a hardcoded assignment:

`this._hasSbomPermission = true;`


This bypasses the actual permission check and always enables SBOM permission in the UI regardless of the user's real project permissions.

## Expected Behavior

- SBOM permission should rely only on the result returned from `hasProjectPermissions(...)`

- Users without SBOM permission should not receive access through the UI

- The temporary hardcoded override should be removed

## Possible Fix

Remove:

```ts

this._hasSbomPermission = true;

```

and verify the permission flow with different project roles/users.

issues.title: Add descriptive alt text to images
issues.body: ### Issue Summary Images in the app installation wizard and two-factor modal have empty `alt=""` attributes, making them inaccessible to screen readers and failing WCAG compliance. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Go to `https://app.cal.com/apps` 2. Click Install on any app 3. You'll see the installation wizard with avatar images 4. Open DevTools (F12) and inspect the avatar images 5. Check the alt attribute - it's empty ### Actual Results -Images have `alt=""` (empty alt text) - Screen readers cannot announce what the image represents - Accessibility checker tools show warnings ### Expected Results - Images should have descriptive alt text like `alt=" admin's avatar"` or `alt="8x8 app icon"` - Screen readers can properly announce the image content ### Evidence

issues.title: Fix inconsistent default port between server.js, Readme.md, and Dockerfile

## Problem
The project has inconsistent port documentation across multiple files:

- **server.js** defaults to port **4321** (`process.env.PORT || 4321`)
- **Readme.md** documented port **8080** in the running locally section
- **Dockerfile** exposed port **8080**

All documentation and configuration should consistently reference the same default port (4321).

issues.title: Fix: Landing Page Not Responsive on Mobile Devices
issues.body:
### Issue Description

The FireForm landing page  is not properly responsive on mobile and tablet devices. The layout breaks and becomes difficult to use on smaller screens, resulting in a poor user experience for visitors accessing the application from phones or tablets.

### Problems Identified

1. **Fixed Padding** 
   - Header has hard-coded `px-12` padding that doesn't adapt to mobile screens
   - Content gets cramped on small devices

2. **Horizontal Navigation Overflow** 
   - Navigation menu items don't fit on mobile
   - Causes horizontal scrolling and layout breaks
   - All nav items force overflow

3. **No Mobile Menu** 
   - Mobile users see all navigation items crammed together
   - No dropdown/hamburger option for mobile
   - Poor usability on small screens

4. **Inflexible Text Sizing** 
   - Headings and text don't scale appropriately for different screen sizes
   - Text too large on mobile, cutting off content
   - Heading stays at `text-5xl` regardless of screen size

5. **Button Layout Issues** 
   - Login button doesn't adapt to mobile screen width
   - Button text too long for mobile displays
   - Fixed padding breaks responsive design

6. **Missing Menu Toggle** 
   - No way to collapse navigation on mobile devices
   - Users must scroll through all navigation items

issues.title: [✨ FEATURE]: Add Back / Review button on Final Submission screen
issues.body: ### Feature Description 📝 Add a (Back) button on the Final Message / Submit screen. This button should take the user back to the previous step so they can review or edit their answers before submitting. ### Motivation 🌟 Right now, once users reach the final screen, they only see the Submit button. If they want to quickly check or fix any answer, there is no clear way to go back. Adding a Back / Review option will prevent accidental submissions and give users more confidence before completing the test. ### Expected Behavior 🤔 a) The final submission screen should show two buttons: 1) ← Review answers 2) Submit b) When the user clicks Review answers, they are taken back to the previous step. c) All previously filled answers should remain saved. d) The user should be able to edit their responses and then return to the final screen to submit. ### Additional Information ℹ️ here in left side of submit button we can have a back /review btn.

issues.title: [✨ FEATURE]: Add “Remove Profile Picture” button option for better user experience
issues.body:
### Feature Description 📝

Currently, users can upload or change their profile picture from the Edit Profile, but there is no option to remove an existing profile picture.
Once a profile picture is uploaded, the only available action is to replace it, which limits user control over their profile.

### Motivation 🌟

Currently, users can upload/change their profile picture, but there is no option
to remove it and revert to the default avatar.

For a better user experience, there should be a clear "Remove Profile Picture"
An option so users can undo accidental uploads.

This is a common UX pattern that would improve the usability of profile customisation.

### Expected Behavior 🤔

 1) A clear "Remove Profile Picture option should be available in the Edit Profile section.
 2) The change should be saved only after the user confirms by clicking "Save Changes".
